chat.action.flyingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven camera move. Preserve {coords}. Keep it brief — screen readers read this aloud.
chat.action.navigatingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven navigation. Preserve {label}. Keep it brief.
chat.action.seekingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven date seek. Preserve {date}. Keep it brief.
chat.autoLoad.altHint: Appended to chat.autoLoad.message when alternative datasets are available. Begins with a leading space; preserve the space if your language uses spaces between sentences.
chat.autoLoad.message: Sent when Orbit auto-loads a dataset on the user's first turn. Preserve **{title}** (markdown bold, rendered) and {altHint} (which is either chat.autoLoad.altHint or empty).
chat.fallback.localDev: Shown when the LLM is unreachable in local development. Backticks render as inline code; preserve them around `npm run dev` and `/api`.
chat.fallback.production: Shown when the LLM is unreachable in production. Brief; the user has no way to fix this themselves.
chat.welcome.hint: Companion line below chat.welcome.intro. Same warm, helpful, non-corporate voice as Orbit. Mentions Browse as a parallel path.
chat.welcome.intro: First-person introduction from Orbit, the AI docent. Voice should be warm and helpful, not corporate. Markdown bold (**Orbit**) renders at runtime; preserve the asterisks.
disclosureBanner.text: First-launch privacy notice. Plain text, no markup. Shown once before any telemetry events fire. Should reassure without being legalistic.
docent.greeting.1: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit when chat opens. First-person, warm, references Earth-science topics. The user has not yet typed anything.
docent.greeting.2: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit. Same constraints as docent.greeting.1.
docent.greeting.3: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit. Same constraints as docent.greeting.1.
docent.help: Orbit's response when the user asks 'what can you do?'. Markdown formatting (bullets, bold) renders at runtime. Mentions specific capabilities — keep examples relevant to Earth-science exploration.
docent.search.fallbackResults: Sent when the user's exact query had no match but a single keyword did. Preserve {query} (the original) and {word} (the matched keyword).
docent.search.multipleMatches: Sent when the user's query matches multiple datasets. Preserve {count}, {query}, **{title}**, {desc}, {qa}. Markdown renders.
docent.search.none: Sent when no datasets match. Preserve {query}. Suggests broad fallback topics; localize the topic suggestions naturally.
docent.search.singleMatch: Sent when one dataset matches the user's query. Preserve **{title}**, {desc}, {qa}. Markdown renders.
docent.system.respondInLanguage: LLM system prompt fragment injected before every chat turn. Preserve <<LOAD:DATASET_ID>> markers verbatim — they are parsed by the runtime tool-call detector. {language} is the user's locale name. The translation IS what the model reads, so phrase it as the instruction you want followed.
docent.system.respondInLanguageReminder: Anchored language reminder injected just before the user's turn. Same constraints as docent.system.respondInLanguage — preserve <<LOAD:...>> markers and {language}.
help.guide.section.exploring: Help-guide HTML blob. Sanitized at runtime, but keep the HTML structure (h3, ul, li, strong, kbd, a tags) intact — translate only the text content. The runtime allowlist rejects script-class tags.
help.guide.section.multiglobe: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.navigating: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.orbit: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.privacy: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring. The link to /privacy and 'Tools → Privacy' reference must stay accurate.
help.guide.section.shortcuts: Help-guide HTML blob about keyboard shortcuts. Preserve <kbd> tags; the literal key names (Esc, Enter, Space) should localize to whatever your language calls those keys.
help.guide.section.tools: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring. Preserve the strong-tagged section names (View, Layout, Clear, Orbit settings) but translate them to match the corresponding tools.* strings.
help.guide.section.tours: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
playback.error.dateOutsideRange: Error shown when a seek lands outside the dataset's time range. Preserve {date}, {start}, {end} placeholders verbatim.
playback.seekingTo: Live-region announcement when seeking to a date. Preserve {date}. Brief.
tour.image.alt: Alt text for tour-embedded images. Describes the image to screen-reader users; not visible text on screen.
tour.legend.alt: Alt text for tour legend images. Describes the legend; not visible text.
tour.question.alt: Alt text for tour question icons. Indicates this is a question prompt; not visible text.
tour.question.answer.alt: Alt text for tour answer icons. Indicates this is an answer; not visible text.
tour.question.answer.aria: ARIA label for tour answer buttons. {n} is the answer number (1, 2, 3…). Read aloud by screen readers when the button is focused; not visible text.
tour.question.loading: Placeholder text shown briefly on the VR tour-question overlay while the question image is still being fetched. Keep short — drawn into a fixed canvas.
vr.controllerTooltip.grip: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the grip button does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand. 'Grip' refers to the squeeze button on the side of a VR controller.
vr.controllerTooltip.thumbstick: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the thumbstick does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand.
vr.controllerTooltip.trigger: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the trigger does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand. 'Trigger' refers to the index-finger button on a VR controller.
